sites.notes:
This is a Commonwealth government program (Attorney-General's Department), not a single place-based community site. Data quality is moderate: figures and people are verified from two official ministerial media releases and corroborating search snippets of the AG.gov.au page.

LOGO: logo_url is null. The AG.gov.au page timed out on every WebFetch attempt (3x), so I could not inspect its HTML head for og:image/favicon. As a Commonwealth department, branding is the Commonwealth Coat of Arms rather than a program-specific logo; no dedicated program logo was found in the public record.

FUNDING FIGURE NUANCE: The program commitment is reported at two figures across sources. The original/initial commitment is "$69 million over 4 years (from 2022-23)" plus "$20 million per year from 2026-27 ongoing" plus "$10 million over 4 years" for Central Australia (2023-24 Budget). The 2024 announcement and search snippets cite a headline "$79 million" total. Both are reported here with their exact source quotes; I did not reconcile or invent a single combined figure.

INITIATIVES COUNT: 26 funded as of 30 Sept 2024 (target up to 30); grant rounds closed Nov 2024 near full subscription. Newer/total count beyond 26 was not confirmed in a primary source, so it is not asserted.

EVALUATION: A search snippet (attributed to AG.gov.au content) states the first evaluation applying the program framework "will begin later in 2026" and that the National Justice Reinvestment Unit was designed via four roundtables (Perth, Adelaide, Darwin, Cairns) in July-Aug 2025. I could not load the AG page directly to verify this verbatim, so it is recorded in history/notes as inferred-from-search rather than as an impact_metric.

CLOSING THE GAP: Program aligns with Closing the Gap Targets 10 and 11 (reducing First Nations adult and youth incarceration). No specific outcome statistics (e.g. reduction percentages) were found in the public record checked.

CULTURAL SAFETY: Only publicly-named ministers/officials are listed in people. No community-level individuals named; partner organisations listed are publicly named in official media releases.

Two grants.gov.au / communitygrants.gov.au pages returned HTTP 403 and could not be read for per-grant funding ranges.

sites.notes:
CONFIRMED: olabuddoogethu.org.au is the org's own site. Logo URL verified by inspecting homepage HTML (header img wp-content/uploads/2020/12/olabud2.png) and confirmed reachable via HTTP 200, Content-Type image/png, 317KB. The smaller favicon variant is .../2020/09/logor.png.

MEANING: "Olabud Doogethu" is Kriol for "all of us together." It is WA's first Justice Reinvestment Site, led by the Shire of Halls Creek with ten/eleven Kimberley Aboriginal communities (Balgo/Wirrimanu, Billiluna/Mindibungu, Mulan, Ringer Soak/Kundat Djaru, Warmun/Turkey Creek, Yiyili, Red Hill, Mardiwah Loop, Yardgee, Nicholson Block).

IMPACT METRICS: All six crime-reduction figures are quoted verbatim and all carry the SAME source_url (the org's own 'Towards a Brighter Halls Creek' page), which a WebFetch confirmed contains them word-for-word. These are 2017-20 / 2018-20 / 2019-20 period figures, so 'year' is set to 2020 (the period end), not a single fresh year. I could NOT verify a primary-source URL containing the verbatim text for the often-cited $1.5M state and $79M federal justice-reinvestment funding figures, so per the hard rules I OMITTED them from impact_metrics rather than attach an unconfirmed source.

PEOPLE / CULTURAL SAFETY: Only two publicly named individuals retained. Mitchell Bradshaw (YENO) is named on the org's own public page. Michael Ogilvie (former chair) appears via a government media-release reference reporting his 2023 move to Social Reinvestment WA — treat as secondary-source/unverified on the org site. I did NOT search for or name community members beyond publicly self-published staff, out of cultural-safety caution; no current coordinator/CEO name was found in the public record during this pass, so people data is thin. If any named person is the subject of Sorry Business, they should be removed on request.

DATA QUALITY = moderate: rich on model description, communities, and the headline 2017-20 outcome figures (all single-sourced to the org's own site, not an independent evaluation); thin on (a) independent/recent evaluation data, (b) verifiable funding dollar figures, (c) current named leadership, and (d) 2024-2026 news (searches surfaced mostly 2019-2023 material). Two key Shire-of-Halls-Creek pages returned 403 and a Ministers' release timed out, limiting cross-verification.
